authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(88) — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Martis Lake tributaries)
sources.quotedText: (88) Martis Lake tributaries (Nevada and Placer Cos.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(87) — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Martis Creek below the dam; establishes the Martis Lake catch-and-release system)
sources.quotedText: (87) Martis Creek from the Martis Lake dam downstream to the confluence with the Truckee River (Nevada Co.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(a)(1) — General Provisions (waters listed in subsection (b) closed except during the shown open season; gear restrictions apply to all species)
sources.quotedText: Every body of water listed in subsection (b) is closed to all fishing except during the open season as shown. Unless otherwise provided, waters shown as open to trout fishing in subsection (b), are open to fishing for other species. Gear restrictions listed in this section apply to the take of all species of fish unless otherwise noted.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Schaeffer Creek / Martis Lake tributaries not listed)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: OpenStreetMap way 649203085 — named waterway "Schaeffer Creek" (waterway=stream), Placer Co., CA. Not present in USGS GNIS under any spelling variant (no gaz_name match in the National Map geonames Streams layer for CA). Traced via Overpass from its headwaters near Sawtooth/Waddle Ranch north to its confluence with Martis Creek at approximately 39.275379, -120.171633 (near Martis Camp, Placer Co.; county confirmed by Nominatim reverse geocoding). Corrects the prior file coordinate (-120.16517, 39.26417), which lay roughly 1.3 km east of the creek's actual mapped course.

groups.humanSummary: Schaeffer Creek is a Placer County tributary of Martis Creek: it flows north and joins Martis Creek (at roughly 39.2754, -120.1716) above Martis Creek Lake (Martis Lake, at roughly 39.321, -120.115), so it feeds Martis Lake via Martis Creek and is a 'Martis Lake tributary' under §7.50(b)(88) ('Nevada and Placer Cos.'). It is therefore governed by the Martis Lake catch-and-release regime — open all year, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, 0 trout — NOT the general trout stream season. Note: Schaeffer Creek is in the Truckee River watershed (Martis Creek drains to the Truckee River below the Martis Lake dam per §7.50(b)(87)); it does NOT drain into Lake Tahoe, so the Lake Tahoe tributary rules do not apply to it.
groups.verbatimText: (88) Martis Lake tributaries (Nevada and Placer Cos.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.note: As a Martis Lake tributary (§7.50(b)(88)), Schaeffer Creek is open all year but catch-and-release only (0 trout) with artificial lures and barbless hooks. Unlike the Lake Tahoe tributaries — which are closed except for a Saturday-before-Memorial-Day-through-Sept-30 window under §7.50(b)(152) — the Martis Lake tributaries carry no seasonal closure; the shown open season is 'All year' (§7.50(a)(1): waters listed in (b) are open only during the season shown, which here is all year).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, catch-and-release only (Martis Lake tributary special regulation).
regulations.verbatimText: (88) Martis Lake tributaries (Nevada and Placer Cos.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures with barbless hooks only; no bait (gear restriction applies to all species per §7.50(a)(1)).
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Schaeffer Creek is wholly within California (Placer Co.); no Nevada reciprocity applies.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+.
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
